meta.certificationName: AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate (MLA-C01)
meta.title: AWS-MLE-2: Preparation, Orchestration, and Production Monitoring

domains.name: Deployment and Orchestration of ML Workflows

questions.stem:
A data science team needs a managed way to run feature engineering and model evaluation code before a training job starts.

Which SageMaker capability is the BEST fit?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because SageMaker Processing is the managed service for preprocessing and evaluation jobs.

**B** is incorrect because Batch Transform is for offline inference, not pre-training processing.

**C** is incorrect because Model Monitor is for post-deployment or ongoing monitoring.

**D** is incorrect because automatic model tuning searches hyperparameters rather than running preprocessing code.
questions.reference: [Data transformation workloads with SageMaker Processing - Amazon SageMaker AIData transformation workloads with SageMaker Processing - Amazon SageMaker AI](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/processing-job.html)

questions.stem:

Which SageMaker capability should the team use?
questions.explanation:

**A** is incorrect because Batch Transform is for offline inference, not pre-training processing.

**C** is incorrect because automatic model tuning searches hyperparameters rather than running preprocessing code.

**D** is incorrect because Model Monitor is for post-deployment or ongoing monitoring.
questions.reference: [Data transformation workloads with SageMaker Processing - Amazon SageMaker AIData transformation workloads with SageMaker Processing - Amazon SageMaker AI](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/processing-job.html)

questions.stem:
A platform team wants reusable features available for batch training and low-latency online inference.

Which service is MOST appropriate?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Feature Store provides managed online and offline feature storage.

**B** is incorrect because Experiments tracks runs rather than serving reusable features.

**C** is incorrect because Pipelines orchestrates workflows but is not the feature store.

**D** is incorrect because CloudWatch monitors resources and metrics, not feature values.
questions.reference: [Create, store, and share features with Feature Store - Amazon SageMaker AICreate, store, and share features with Feature Store - Amazon SageMaker AI](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/feature-store.html)

questions.stem:
An ML engineer must join and transform large raw datasets before model training, while maintaining a metadata catalog for repeatable ETL.

Which AWS service is the BEST fit?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because AWS Glue is AWS’s managed ETL and catalog service for data preparation workflows.

**B** is incorrect because CloudWatch is for monitoring.

**C** is incorrect because Model Registry governs model versions, not ETL.

**D** is incorrect because CodeDeploy is not the primary service for analytical ETL.
questions.reference: [What is AWS Glue? - AWS GlueWhat is AWS Glue? - AWS Glue](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/glue/latest/dg/what-is-glue.html)

questions.stem:
A team wants to compare training runs, parameters, and metrics from multiple SageMaker experiments.

Which SageMaker capability is MOST appropriate?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because SageMaker Experiments tracks and compares model-development runs.

**B** is incorrect because Batch Transform is for inference on batch data.

**C** is incorrect because EventBridge routes events rather than tracking experiments.

**D** is incorrect because Lambda layers are unrelated to experiment tracking.
questions.reference: [Amazon SageMaker Experiments in Studio Classic - Amazon SageMaker AIAmazon SageMaker Experiments in Studio Classic - Amazon SageMaker AI](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/experiments.html)

questions.stem:
A team wants to store approved model versions and move them through governance gates before deployment.

Which SageMaker capability is MOST appropriate?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Model Registry manages model package versions and approval workflows.

**B** is incorrect because Data Wrangler is for data preparation.

**C** is incorrect because notebook instances are development environments, not governance registries.

**D** is incorrect because Glue crawlers catalog data rather than model versions.

questions.stem:
A training job needs automated search over hyperparameter combinations to improve model performance.

Which SageMaker capability is MOST appropriate?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because automatic model tuning is SageMaker’s managed hyperparameter optimization capability.

**B** is incorrect because Model Monitor observes deployed models.

**C** is incorrect because Feature Store manages features rather than tuning runs.

**D** is incorrect because serverless inference is a hosting option, not a tuning feature.

questions.stem:
An organization wants an end-to-end ML workflow with processing, training, evaluation, conditional approval, and registration steps.

Which SageMaker capability is the BEST fit?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because SageMaker Pipelines orchestrates end-to-end ML workflows with purpose-built steps.

**B** is incorrect because S3 lifecycle manages storage objects, not ML workflow logic.

**C** is incorrect because Session Manager is unrelated to ML orchestration.

**D** is incorrect because the Glue catalog alone does not orchestrate the full ML lifecycle.
questions.reference: [Pipelines - Amazon SageMaker AIPipelines - Amazon SageMaker AI](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/pipelines.html)

questions.stem:
A model receives occasional very large payloads and can take several minutes to produce each prediction.

Which deployment pattern is MOST appropriate?

options.text: Real-time endpoint with the smallest instance
Batch Transform only if clients need immediate responses
Serverless inference with a strict sub-second expectation

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because asynchronous inference is designed for large payloads or long processing times.

**B** is incorrect because small real-time endpoints are a poor fit for long-running requests.

**C** is incorrect because Batch Transform is offline rather than request/response.

**D** is incorrect because serverless is not the preferred choice for long-running asynchronous jobs.
questions.reference: [Asynchronous inference - Amazon SageMaker AIAsynchronous inference - Amazon SageMaker AI](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/async-inference.html)

questions.stem:
A production team wants to detect data drift and model quality issues after a model is deployed.

Which SageMaker capability is MOST appropriate?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Model Monitor is designed for ongoing checks such as data and model quality drift.

**B** is incorrect because Processing is for standalone jobs rather than deployment monitoring.

**C** is incorrect because notebooks are development tools, not managed drift monitors.

**D** is incorrect because Glue Studio is for data integration, not deployed model monitoring.
questions.reference: [Data and model quality monitoring with Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor - Amazon SageMaker AIData and model quality monitoring with Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor - Amazon SageMaker AI](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/model-monitor.html)

questions.stem:
A responsible AI review requires bias analysis and explainability for model behavior before and after deployment.

Which SageMaker capability is MOST appropriate?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because SageMaker Clarify provides bias detection and explainability workflows.

**B** is incorrect because Batch Transform performs offline predictions, not bias analysis.

**C** is incorrect because Athena queries data but does not provide model bias explanations.

**D** is incorrect because AWS Config records resource configuration changes rather than ML explainability.
questions.reference: [Pre-training Data Bias - Amazon SageMaker AIPre-training Data Bias - Amazon SageMaker AI](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/clarify-detect-data-bias.html)
